title: Workflow Node Types
description: Registered Flow canvas node types, source-derived from the workflow-engine node registry.

items.name: HTTP Request
items.description: Make an HTTP/HTTPS request to any external API. Supports GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH, custom headers, JSON or string bodies, templating, and per-request timeout.

items.name: Webhook Response
items.description: Send a response back to the webhook caller. Sets the HTTP status code, headers, and body for the inbound webhook that triggered this workflow.

items.name: A2A (Agent-to-Agent)
items.description: Agent-to-Agent protocol — alias of agent_spawn that takes a 'prompt' (instead of 'task') and routes through openagentic-proxy.

items.name: Agent Pool
items.description: Run a fixed list of agents in parallel through openagentic-proxy. Aggregates their results with the configured strategy (merge/first/vote).

items.name: Single Agent
items.description: Run one agent through openagentic-proxy with orchestration='parallel'. Returns the unwrapped output plus the per-agent results array.

items.name: Agent Spawn
items.description: Spawn a single agent through openagentic-proxy. The proxy resolves the agent role to a DB-backed agent record and runs the task.

items.name: Agent Supervisor
items.description: Run a supervisor + worker pool through openagentic-proxy with orchestration='supervisor'. The supervisor delegates subtasks to workers.

items.name: Aggregate (LLM)
items.description: LLM-driven reduce over an array. Two modes: `reduce` (single LLM call with the entire array serialized into the prompt → one summary string + one usage entry) and `map` (N LLM calls, one per item → array of N results). Uses the SAME /v1/chat/completions endpoint as llm_completion

items.name: Azure OpenAI
items.description: Send a prompt through Azure OpenAI via the platform's OpenAI-compatible chat-completion endpoint (provider:'azure_openai').

items.name: AWS Bedrock
items.description: Send a prompt through AWS Bedrock via the platform's OpenAI-compatible chat-completion endpoint (provider:'bedrock').

items.name: Conversation Memory
items.description: Read / write / clear / summarize / semantic-search a tenant-scoped chat conversation history. Backs stateful agents and HITL flows that span multiple user turns.

items.name: Grounding Check
items.description: Verify that an LLM-produced claim references only entities that appear in one or more upstream ground-truth sources. Extracts pod-like / component-like tokens from the claim and intersects with tokens present in the truth sources. Deterministic, no LLM round-trip. Use BEFORE noti

items.name: Guardrails
items.description: Validate content against safety rules (PII, toxicity, prompt-injection). Calls the platform's DLP scanner API; falls back to local regex on failure.

items.name: LLM Completion
items.description: Send a prompt to a chat-completion model via the platform's OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Supports system + user messages, automatic context injection, and Smart Router model selection.

items.name: LLM Router
items.description: LLM-as-condition. Given a prompt + a configured set of named routes (each with a description), the model picks exactly one route by name. The chosen route is used as the outgoing `sourceHandle` so downstream branches gate naturally. Goes through the SAME /v1/chat/completions endp

items.name: Multi-Agent
items.description: Concurrent fan-out across N agents via openagentic-proxy. Falls back to direct LLM batch when openagentic-proxy is unavailable.

items.name: Multi-Query Expand
items.description: Expand one question into N retrieval query variants to widen RAG recall (the 'multi-query retriever' pattern). The default path is a DETERMINISTIC rule-based expander — it derives reformulations (keyword-only, question→statement, synonym/paraphrase prefixes, and the original) wit

items.name: OpenAgentic Chat
items.description: Conversational LLM via the platform's Smart Router. Same shape as openagentic_llm — supports modelOverride, sliderPosition, and chain-of-thought thinking.

items.name: Prompt Template
items.description: Build a reusable prompt with {{variable}} placeholders. Outputs either a single rendered string ('prompt' mode) or a structured chat conversation split on {{system}} / {{user}} / {{assistant}} role markers ('messages' mode).

items.name: Reasoning
items.description: Extended chain-of-thought via the platform's Smart Router. Forces enableThinking:true at max quality (sliderPosition:100). Use for hard problems.

items.name: Rerank Chunks
items.description: Re-order a list of retrieved chunks by relevance to a query using a deterministic lexical relevance scorer (BM25-style term-overlap with IDF weighting + phrase-match boost). No model round-trip on the default path, so it is deterministic and harness-friendly. Optionally an LLM cr

items.name: Structured Output
items.description: Force the LLM to emit valid JSON matching the supplied schema. Retries on parse failure.

items.name: Google Vertex AI
items.description: Send a prompt through Google Vertex AI via the platform's OpenAI-compatible chat-completion endpoint (provider:'vertex').

items.name: Text Annotation
items.description: Visual annotation node — passes input through untouched. Useful for documentation/comments embedded in the canvas.

items.name: Condition
items.description: Evaluate a JavaScript expression and route to the matching downstream branch (true/false or custom labels).

items.name: Deduplicate
items.description: Idempotency gate. Computes a key from the configured expression and drops (or errors on) items whose key has already been seen — within the current execution by default, or across executions with an optional TTL window.

items.name: Error Handler
items.description: Receives routed errors from upstream nodes and handles them via log, notify, transform, or retry actions.

items.name: Flow as Tool
items.description: Wrap a saved Flow as a callable tool. Maps caller args to the wrapped flow's trigger input via inputMapping, runs the flow as a sub-execution, and extracts a value at outputExtract from the result. V1: callable from a parent flow; agent dynamic-tool integration is V1.1.

items.name: Human Approval
items.description: Pause the workflow until a configured approver accepts or rejects. The engine returns control downstream once an approval decision is recorded, or applies the configured timeout action if the timeout elapses first.

items.name: Request Data from User
items.description: Pause the workflow and ask the user to fill a typed form, then resume with their answers. Unlike human_approval (which only yields approve/reject), human_input collects actual values mid-run — a missing parameter, a decision the model can't make, or a credential to bind. Downstre

items.name: Loop
items.description: Iterate over a collection by re-executing the downstream subgraph once per item, with the iteration variable bound in the per-iteration input.

items.name: Map / Reduce
items.description: Fan out over an input collection — run the downstream subgraph once per item under a concurrency limit — then reduce the per-item results into a single value (collect, concat, sum, avg, min, max, count).

items.name: Merge
items.description: Combine results from multiple incoming branches into a single output using array, object, or concat strategy.

items.name: Parallel
items.description: Fan out to all outgoing branches and execute them in parallel. Returns each branch's result + an aggregate success rate.

items.name: Rate Limiter
items.description: Fixed-window throttle for fan-out to rate-limited APIs. Per-(tenant, key) counter; choose block (sleep until reset), drop (return without invoking), or error (throw) on overflow.

items.name: Retry with Backoff
items.description: Wraps a downstream operation and retries it with exponential backoff on failure. Returns the first successful result; throws a clear error naming the last failure once all retries are exhausted.

items.name: Sub-workflow
items.description: Invoke another saved workflow by id and inline its output. Lets you compose flows the way you compose functions.

items.name: Switch
items.description: Evaluate an expression and route to the matching case branch. Supports a default fallback case.

items.name: Wait
items.description: Pause workflow execution for a specified duration. Short waits (< 30 s) sleep in-process; long waits store state and schedule a resume.

items.name: Wait For Condition
items.description: Poll an expression until it becomes truthy (or a timeout elapses). Differs from `wait`, which sleeps a fixed duration. Useful for: 'wait until job_status == done', 'wait until pod ready', 'wait until queue depth < N'. Reference: n8n's Wait node 'until condition' variant + the n8n

items.name: Anomaly Detect
items.description: Statistical anomaly detection over a time-series window. Flags outliers and exposes a top-level hasAnomaly verdict for downstream branch nodes (policy_guard, runbook_executor, etc.).

items.name: CSV Processor
items.description: Parse CSV text into records (objects keyed by header) or rows (string arrays). V1 ships text-mode only; binary/file mode is a follow-up after the binary data plane lands.

items.name: Data Query
items.description: Run a vector / collection query against a configured datasource. Returns the matching results so downstream nodes can summarise, rank, or feed an LLM.

items.name: Data Source Query
items.description: Run a query against a configured DataSource. Supports raw SQL/REST queries (mode='raw') and natural-language questions that the platform translates to SQL (mode='nl'). Distinct from data_query which performs vector search.

items.name: Document Loader
items.description: Fetch a document from a URL (or pass through inline input). Optional parseMode strips HTML or pretty-prints JSON.

items.name: Embedding
items.description: Generate vector embeddings for one or more texts via the platform's /api/v1/embeddings endpoint. Falls back to /api/v1/vector/embed on primary failure.

items.name: Extract Key
items.description: Pull a single value at a dot/bracket path from an object, with optional default. Replaces the transform-extract case for the common 'give me steps.X.data.id' pattern.

items.name: File Upload (Embed)
items.description: Upload content for chunking + embedding into a Milvus collection via the platform's /api/files/embed endpoint.

items.name: Filter Array
items.description: Filter an array by a typed field predicate. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for the common 'keep rows where X' case.

items.name: Knowledge Ingest
items.description: Ingest content into a Milvus knowledge base via the platform's /api/chat/knowledge/ingest endpoint.

items.name: Knowledge Search
items.description: Semantic search against the platform knowledge base (shared_knowledge + the calling user's private collection) via /api/chat/knowledge/search. Returns ranked chunks merged across both. Pair this with knowledge_ingest: the ingest endpoint writes to shared_knowledge / user_<id>_pri

items.name: Parse JSON
items.description: Parse a JSON string into a structured object. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for the common 'LLM returned a JSON string' case.

items.name: RAG Query
items.description: Run a semantic search against a Milvus collection via the platform's /api/v1/vector/search endpoint. Returns ranked results with optional score threshold.

items.name: Regex
items.description: Apply a regex pattern to a string. Supports match, replace, and test modes. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for pattern extraction and substitution.

items.name: Save File
items.description: Persist arbitrary text content to the artifact store (MinIO/GCS/Azure via the platform's BlobStorageService). Returns {artifactId, url, sizeBytes, mimeType}. Text payloads only for now — binary mode lands when the binary plane (Tier 2 #5) is wired through. For string-valued flow

items.name: Select Fields
items.description: Pick or omit specific fields by dot-path from an object or each row of an array. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for the common 'project these columns' case.

items.name: Text Splitter
items.description: Split text into chunks for embedding. Strategies: recursive (separator-aware) or fixed (fixed-size). Runs in-process, no HTTP.

items.name: Transform
items.description: Apply map, filter, reduce, or extract operations to the input using sandboxed JavaScript expressions.

items.name: User Context
items.description: Inject the calling user's profile, memories, and recent chats into the workflow. Calls the platform's /api/user-context endpoint.

items.name: Vector Store
items.description: Upsert or delete vectors in a Milvus collection via the platform's /api/v1/vector/store endpoint. Falls back to /api/files/embed when the primary endpoint fails.

items.name: Discord Message
items.description: Send a message to a Discord channel via webhook. Supports plain content, custom username, and embeds.

items.name: Jira Issue
items.description: Create a Jira issue via REST API v3. Auth via JIRA_BASE_URL + JIRA_EMAIL + JIRA_API_TOKEN env vars.

items.name: MCP Tool
items.description: Invoke an MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool through the platform's mcp-proxy. Auth runs as the user (OBO federation for AWS/Azure).

items.name: Outlook Email
items.description: Send an email via Microsoft 365 / Outlook SMTP (smtp.office365.com:587). Credentials read from OUTLOOK_USER / OUTLOOK_PASSWORD env vars (or SMTP_USER / SMTP_PASS fallback).

items.name: PagerDuty Incident
items.description: Trigger / acknowledge / resolve a PagerDuty incident via Events API v2. Supports dedupKey, severity, custom_details, and client/client_url fields.

items.name: Send Email (SMTP)
items.description: Send email via SMTP. Requires explicit per-node credentials — either literal values or {{secret:NAME}} workflow-secret references. No env-var fallback (2026-05-15 hardening: every flow that sends mail must carry its own credentials).

items.name: ServiceNow Ticket
items.description: Create a ServiceNow record via the REST Table API. Defaults to creating an incident; supports any configurable table (change_request, problem, etc).

items.name: Slack Message
items.description: Send a message to a Slack channel via incoming webhook. Supports templated text, channel override, and Block Kit blocks.

items.name: Splunk
items.description: Run SPL searches, acknowledge notable events, or push events via HEC against Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud REST APIs.

items.name: Microsoft Teams Message
items.description: Post a message to a Microsoft Teams channel via incoming webhook. Supports plain text or Adaptive Cards.

items.name: Trigger
items.description: Workflow entry point. Emits the input as the first event so downstream nodes can reference it via {{trigger.body.*}} and {{trigger.<key>}}.
